text.title: GCS and Cloud CDN Configuration Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the gcp-storage-cdn methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Set lifecycle rules on every production bucket: NEARLINE after 30 days idle, COLDLINE after 90, optionally ARCHIVE after 365.
rule.rationale: Lifecycle rules are the single highest-leverage GCS cost lever; absence means full STANDARD cost forever.

rule.statement: Enable object versioning on buckets that hold mutable critical data and cap retained versions at 3-5 via lifecycle.
rule.rationale: Versioning is the cheap recovery path for accidental overwrite; uncapped versions become a runaway cost.

rule.statement: Apply a retention policy + bucket lock on regulated buckets (audit, finance, healthcare) sized to the legal hold period.
rule.rationale: Bucket lock is the only WORM mechanism GCS exposes; retention policy without lock is advisory.

rule.statement: On Cloud CDN, set custom cache keys explicitly including host + path + query allow-list, never the default 'include everything'.
rule.rationale: Loose cache keys split or duplicate cache entries — wrecking hit-rate and amplifying origin cost.

rule.statement: Serve user-uploaded or sensitive assets via signed URLs / signed cookies with short expiry; never make those buckets public.
rule.rationale: Public buckets are the dominant GCS leakage source; signed-URL flows are the documented safe default.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
